summary.intent: Improve the Gossamer app's performance and UX: eliminate the inefficient repoForCheckpoint pattern, fix missing planning messages in SessionDetail, rework the repos/BranchLog screens to look like SessionDetail, and fix BranchLog slowness via lazy loading and pagination.
summary.outcome: All major goals achieved: localPath-based checkpoint routes deployed, planning text folded into thinking blocks, BranchLog rewritten with git-log-based pagination (50 commits/page) and lazy log event loading per checkpoint, breadcrumbs cleaned up, diff line-number sticky scroll bug fixed, and composite DB indexes added to speed up the sessions list endpoint.

repo: Claude Code writes two separate JSONL events per API response: event A is text-only (planning), event B is tool-use-only with the same message.id but a different uuid — this caused planning text to be silently overwritten in logEventsToEvents
Git commits include an 'Entire-Checkpoint: <id>' trailer line that can be parsed to map commits → checkpoint IDs without any DB join
SQLite has a ~999 bind variable limit; large IN queries on LogEvent (sessionId IN [...]) hit this limit with repos like gossamer that have many sessions
Prisma 7 + better-sqlite3 is the DB layer; WAL mode is already enabled so indexer writes don't block reads
Each repo can have its own .db file; findRepo(localPath) maps path → dbPath

LogEvent rows include relations via LOG_EVENT_INCLUDE (systemData, hookProgress, logContents, logUsage) — fetching these with findMany for all sessions is the primary performance bottleneck in BranchLog
diff2html sets position:sticky on .d2h-code-linenumber and .d2h-code-side-linenumber cells, which misbehaves inside Mantine ScrollArea by anchoring to the wrong scroll container

code.finding: GET /api/branch-log now uses git log --format=%B%x1e --max-count=50 --skip=N to paginate commits, parses Entire-Checkpoint: <id> trailers, and returns only checkpoint metadata (no logEvents) — logEvents are lazy-loaded per session from the frontend

mapLogEvent type must be derived as: type LogEventWithIncludes = Awaited<ReturnType<typeof db.logEvent.findMany<{ include: typeof LOG_EVENT_INCLUDE }>>>[number] — using the base findMany return type omits relations and causes TS2339 errors
When a new text block arrives after tool uses in the same turn (same message.id), fold the earlier text into lastAssistantThinking to prevent it being overwritten by the final summary text
Override .d2h-code-linenumber and .d2h-code-side-linenumber with position:static to prevent sticky anchoring inside Mantine ScrollArea
Added @@index([sessionId, timestamp]) and @@index([sessionId, id]) to LogEvent model to speed up the groupBy min/max and distinct/orderBy queries in GET /api/sessions
GET /api/sessions is the endpoint for the main sessions list; called by fetchSessions() in api.ts:103

workflow: Per-item individual DB lookups (one query per checkpoint) scale better than large IN clauses when the set size is unpredictable
Lazy loading (fetch on expand) is preferable to upfront bulk fetching when list items contain large nested datasets
When a synthetic emit approach is 'too noisy' for the user, folding content into an existing collapsible UI element (thinking block) is a less disruptive alternative

friction: First chunking fix (findManyInChunks) for the SQLite variable limit was discarded entirely — user wanted a completely different architectural approach (git-log-first)
First planning-message fix (synthetic Stop events) was rejected as too noisy — required a second approach
BranchLog went through multiple performance iterations: 200→50 page size, then full lazy-load rewrite, because log event fetching was still happening upfront despite page size reduction
Build error TS2339 on mapLogEvent required understanding Prisma's generic findMany return type to fix correctly
Arrow function syntax was accidentally broken during a BranchLog edit (block body changed to expression body), causing a compile error
open_items: DB indexes added to schema.prisma need to be applied to the live DB via: DATABASE_URL='file:~/.claude/hook-handler.db' npx prisma db push
The sessions list endpoint (GET /api/sessions) may still be slow even after indexes — no profiling was done to confirm the indexes are sufficient
BranchLog hasMore detection is based on git commit count (not checkpoint count) — a page of 50 commits with no checkpoints still shows 'Load more', which will show an empty next page
The uncommitted bug fix (allLogEvents querying repoDb instead of db) from the original branch state was never committed
